text.title: docker-compose Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for docker-compose as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using version: "3.9" at the top of compose.yaml and naming the file docker-compose.yml.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Both are legacy patterns. version: is ignored by modern Compose and adds confusion about which features are available.

failure-mode.description: Running docker compose down -v in production or without explicit confirmation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The -v flag deletes all named volumes including database data. This is irreversible. Agents must never run this command against production without explicit operator confirmation.
